values.name: initState
values.type: Config -> Predicate -> List ( String, OperandType ) -> State

values.name: dropdownCss
values.comment: Default CSS for the dropdown selectors

name: QueryBuilder.Json
QueryBuilder.State
comment:
# State

@docs State, OperandType, PredicateOperator, Predicate, Operand, StringOperand, StringOperator, OptionOperand, NumberOperand, NumberOperator, BooleanOperand, Selector, Selection, Config, Msg

unions.name: NumberOperator
unions.comment: Operator available to query numerical attributes.

unions.name: Operand
unions.comment:
A single condition of the whole predicate used to make the query.

`NoOp` represents a currently undefined operand, while the rest define operands for each `OperandType`.

unions.name: OperandType
unions.comment:
Defines the type of operands that are supported by the query builder.

The `IsOption` type includes all the possible options.

unions.name: PredicateOperator
unions.comment: Logical operator for the predicate, either all operands must be statisfied (`And`) or only a single operand must be satisfied (`Or`).

unions.name: Selection
unions.comment: Represents the possible options for dropdown menus in the query builder.

unions.name: StringOperator
unions.comment: Operators available to query string attributes

aliases.name: BooleanOperand
aliases.comment:
`attribute` is the name of the attribute being queried.
`negated` is whether the query is looking for a match or inverse.
`value` is the value that will be used to match against.
aliases.type: { attribute : String, negated : Bool, value : Bool }

aliases.name: Config
aliases.comment:
Configuration options for the query builder.

`dropdownCss` is optional custom css for the dropdown selector.
`removeClass` is an optional class that will be added to the remove button next to each operand.
`addClass` is an optional class that will be added to the add an operand button.
`allowNegation` determines whether the query builder allows operands to be negated.
aliases.type: { dropdownCss : Maybe (Attribute Msg), removeClass : Maybe String, addClass : Maybe String, allowNegation : Bool }

aliases.name: NumberOperand
aliases.comment:
`attribute` is the name of the attribute being queried.
`operator` is the operation that will be used to make the query.
`negated` is whether the query will return matches or its inverse.
`value` is the value that will be used by the operator in the query.
aliases.type: { attribute : String, operator : NumberOperator, negated : Bool, value : String }

aliases.name: OptionOperand
aliases.comment:
`attribute` is the name of the attribute being queried.
`negated` is whether the query is looking for a match or inverse.
`value` is the value that will be used to match against.
aliases.type: { attribute : String, negated : Bool, value : String }

aliases.name: Predicate
aliases.comment: A predicate is just a list of operands.

aliases.name: Selector
aliases.comment: Represents a dropdown menu in the query builder.
aliases.type: { dropdown : State, dropdownId : String, options : List Selection }

aliases.name: State
aliases.comment: Holds the state of the query builder, including the predicate as well as the state of the dropdowns.
aliases.type: { predicate : Predicate, predicateOperator : PredicateOperator, selectorState : List Selector, allAttributes : List ( String, OperandType ), dropdownConfig : Int -> Config Msg Selection, config : Config, nextId : String }

aliases.name: StringOperand
aliases.comment:
`attribute` is the name of the attribute being queried.
`operator` is the operation that will be used to make the query.
`value` is the value that will be used by the operator in the query.
aliases.type: { attribute : String, operator : StringOperator, value : String }
